text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. One common type is supervised learning. In this method, we give the computer a set of training data, which includes input examples and their correct answers. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules or patterns it discovers. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, it might predict house prices or whether an email is spam. Finally, we check the model's accuracy by comparing its predictions to the actual results. Higher accuracy means better learning.
